reasons: Lolita can protect allies from Layla's front-facing ranged basic attacks and projectile pressure.
Layla wants long-range uptime, while Lolita can force her to wait, reposition, or attack from another angle.

proof.summary: Layla wants to attack safely from long range, while Lolita can reflect incoming ranged basic attacks and projectiles from the shield direction.
worksBestWhen: Lolita faces Layla before Layla commits damage.
Allies stay behind Lolita during siege or objective fights.
failureCases: Layla attacks from a side angle outside the shield.
Lolita's shield is baited before the real teamfight.

reasons: Natalia can threaten Layla from conceal and punish her lack of safe escape.
Layla is dangerous at range but vulnerable when assassins reach her without peel.

proof.summary: Layla wants maximum distance and clear sightlines, while Natalia threatens unseen backline access before Layla can freely attack.
worksBestWhen: Natalia controls bushes before Layla rotates.
Layla is separated from tank or support peel.
failureCases: Natalia is revealed before reaching the backline.

reasons: Saber can punish Layla's immobility with targeted airborne burst.
Layla depends on distance and protection, so she is vulnerable when caught without peel.

proof.summary: Layla wants to stay far away and attack safely, while Saber can close the gap and lock her with airborne burst if she is exposed.
worksBestWhen: Saber attacks from fog or flank angle.
Layla's defensive spell or team peel is unavailable.
failureCases: Layla is protected by tank crowd control.
Saber cannot burst Layla before counter-engage.

reasons: Franco can punish Layla's static backline positioning with hook or suppression.
A successful pick removes Layla's range advantage before the fight starts.

proof.summary: Layla wants to keep distance behind her team, while Franco can punish exposed positioning with hook or suppression from fog.
worksBestWhen: Franco controls bush vision near Layla's rotation.
The team follows up immediately after Layla is hooked.
failureCases: Layla stays behind minions or frontline protection.

reasons: Belerick can punish repeated basic attacks and protect allies from Layla's damage focus.
Layla may struggle to freely hit priority targets when Belerick zones and absorbs damage.

proof.summary: Layla wants to output repeated basic attacks from range, while Belerick can absorb pressure, punish basic-attack focus, and protect teammates.
worksBestWhen: Belerick stands between Layla and the backline.
Belerick has item durability before forcing Layla to hit him.
failureCases: Layla kites Belerick from maximum range.
Belerick cannot reach or zone Layla's attack angle.
